structured_output.summary: Unfading is a private iOS map diary for couples and recurring groups (couple + general_group dual mode). The app is in a release-ready, evaluation-passed state as of 2026-04-08 (release-closure-r3b). All critical blockers have been resolved: test target configuration fixed (10/10 passing), MemoryComposerSheet wired into the app target and presented from the New Pin button, runtime evidence generated for denied location, manual place picker, and large Dynamic Type flows, and stale blackboard contamination cleared. The next phase is optional quality follow-up (dark-mode screenshot, expanded bottom sheet capture, VoiceOver evidence) rather than emergency remediation. Market positioning targets a gap between public social sharing tools and the absent private spatial memory category for small recurring groups.
user_pains: Users cannot answer 'What happened here with us?' from existing tools — chat albums, photo feeds, and map reviews all fail to preserve place-based emotional context around shared events.
Recovering a date course, trip stop, or birthday location requires manually searching years of chat logs with no spatial or event grouping.
Existing memory products are optimized for public sharing or place discovery and do not support private shared memory for recurring groups or couples simultaneously.
There is no single surface that ties photo, place, time, group, and emotion together so a couple or recurring group can revisit layered history at the same location.
Rewind and anniversary moments are lost unless users manually curate them; no product surfaces 'N years ago today' in context with the original photo, place, and participants.
hig_risks: Bottom sheet as a primary exploratory surface must snap predictably and preserve native scroll momentum — custom snap implementations risk breaking UIScrollView gesture coordination and violating the native feel expected from Maps-class panels.
New Pin floating action button must maintain at least 44 pt hit target and clear safe-area margins; oversized or edge-clipped FABs are a known App Store visual review flag.

Map pin clustering and time-filter animations must not conflict with MapKit's native gesture recognizers (pan, pinch, long-press for pin drop) — overlay gesture interceptors that consume touches without passthrough will feel hostile on-device.
Emotion tag multi-select and reaction controls inside memory cards must meet 44 pt minimum; small inline reaction buttons common in social apps frequently fall below this threshold.

Location permission denied recovery flow must use native Settings deep-link (UIApplication.openSettingsURLString) and must not simulate a system alert — fake permission dialogs are an App Store rejection risk.

Dynamic Type scaling in bottom-sheet memory cards and event summary rows must not clip or overlap adjacent elements; fixed-height card containers that ignore preferredContentSizeCategory will fail accessibility review.

Rewind notification scheduling must respect iOS background execution limits and use UNUserNotificationCenter correctly — scheduling more than 64 pending notifications silently drops the oldest, which could cause missed anniversary alerts.
Group invite link flow must not expose deep-link URL schemes that bypass App Store review detection of hidden paid features or referral abuse patterns.
design_references: Naver Map and Google Maps result panels — bottom-sheet confidence, snapping behavior, and spatial hierarchy for the primary browsing surface.
iPhone Photos app — content grouping clarity under date and event headers, memory card density, and grid-to-detail navigation polish.
Apple Maps — calm native map hierarchy, pin and cluster visual language, and safe-area-respecting floating controls.
Private diary apps (Day One, Momento) — intimate, reflective tone and warm typography hierarchy without social-feed noise.
Top-tier iOS productivity apps (Fantastical, Things 3) — motion restraint, purposeful transitions, and predictable navigation stack behavior as the polish benchmark.
Apple Human Interface Guidelines — safe areas, 44 pt targets, Dynamic Type, dark mode contrast, VoiceOver label requirements, and native gesture preservation as non-negotiable release gates.
